goal: Optimize reports and operations navigation with mature-product submenu and contextual search patterns.
deliverable: Shared view and subview navigation model
Contextual second-level menus for reports and operations
Subview-aware deep links and hash routing
Lightweight contextual search for workbench and report queues
constraints: Stay on the current Vue plus FastAPI stack
Avoid ERP-style over-expansion or a global mega menu
Keep the optimization understandable for current operators
acceptance_criteria: Operations and reports expose explicit subviews instead of long-scroll mixed surfaces
Routes support direct opening of a view plus subview pair
Search only appears where list-heavy work actually benefits from retrieval
Baseline validation passes after the UI refactor
product_acceptance_criteria: Operators can switch between overview, work queues, master data, and exceptions without scrolling through unrelated blocks
Report users can focus on tracker or supplier analysis with local search instead of scanning the whole page
manual_spot_checks: Open reports tracker and reports suppliers directly through hash deep links
Search within operations procurement and confirm purchase, receipt, and replenishment cards narrow correctly
Search within reports tracker and supplier analysis and confirm only the relevant rows remain visible
Switch across primary views and confirm old views without submenus still behave normally
completion_language_policy: Only claim completion after validation passes and the new submenu plus search model is wired into the shipped UI.
delivery_truth_contract: Describe only the submenu, hash-routing, and contextual-search changes that were actually implemented and verified.
non_goals: A full information architecture redesign across every page
inferred_assumptions: The user wants practical UI restructuring now, not another abstract plan-only round.
A contextual submenu is preferable to a deeper left navigation tree for this product stage.
